mission.title: Install and Configure Redpanda
mission.description: Redpanda is a high-performance streaming data platform that is Kafka API compatible and designed for simplicity and speed. It eliminates the need for ZooKeeper and JVM, making it easier to operate. Ideal for developers looking for a lightweight alternative to traditional streaming platforms.

steps.title: Add Redpanda Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Redpanda Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add redpanda https://charts.redpanda.com

helm repo update

```

> This adds the Redpanda Helm chart repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Redpanda CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Redpanda to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/redpanda-data/redpanda/v25.3.10/deploy/crds/redpanda.vectorized.io_redpandas.yaml

```

> Installs the Redpanda CRDs.

steps.title: Install Redpanda using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs Redpanda in the specified namespace using Helm.

```bash

helm install redpanda redpanda/redpanda --namespace redpanda --create-namespace --version 25.3.10

```

> Installs Redpanda in the 'redpanda' namespace.

steps.title: Create a Redpanda Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a sample Redpanda cluster configuration.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vectorized.io/v1beta1
kind: Redpanda
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

```

> Creates a Redpanda cluster with 3 replicas.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vectorized.io/v1beta1
kind: Redpanda
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

steps.title: Verify Redpanda Cluster Status
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Redpanda cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get redpandas -n redpanda

```

> Verifies that the Redpanda cluster is up and running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Redpanda
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Redpanda installation and warns about potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall redpanda --namespace redpanda

kubectl delete namespace redpanda

```

> Uninstalls the Redpanda release.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Redpanda
upgrade.description:
This step includes backing up your data, upgrading Redpanda, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get redpandas -n redpanda -o yaml > redpanda-backup.yaml

helm upgrade redpanda redpanda/redpanda --namespace redpanda --version 25.3.10

kubectl get pods -n redpanda

```

> Backs up the current Redpanda configuration.
> Upgrades Redpanda to the specified version.
> Verifies that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get redpandas -n redpanda -o yaml > redpanda-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Redpanda'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Redpanda service is not running or if there are network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Redpanda pods using 'kubectl get pods -n redpanda' and ensure they are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No such host'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Redpanda service is not reachable, possibly due to misconfigured DNS.

**Fix:**
Verify the service endpoints using 'kubectl get svc -n redpanda' and ensure they are correct.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the Kubernetes cluster does not have enough resources to schedule Redpanda pods.

**Fix:**
Check the resource allocation and consider scaling your cluster or adjusting resource requests.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Redpanda pod is crashing repeatedly, possibly due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n redpanda' for more details on the error.

versionNotes.changes: Improved performance with new partitioning strategies and enhanced monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Removed deprecated APIs related to legacy configurations.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 25.2.x, ensure to review the new configuration options in the documentation.

resolution.summary: You should see the Redpanda cluster running with the command 'kubectl get redpandas -n redpanda' showing the status as 'Running'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vectorized.io/v1beta1
kind: Redpanda
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
